title: Iliad, Book 24: The Ransom of Hector
description: Book 24 of Homer's Iliad: The Ransom of Hector.
introduction: Book 24 of the Iliad: The Ransom of Hector. The Greek text is from the Venetus A manuscript (10th century), the oldest complete manuscript of the Iliad.

translation.original_text: Λῦτο δ᾿ ἀγών, λαοὶ δὲ θοὰς ἐπὶ νῆας ἕκαστοι ἐσκίδναντ᾿ ἰέναι.
translation.text: The games broke up and the people scattered. But Achilles could not sleep. Every night he dragged Hector's body around Patroclus' tomb. After twelve days, the gods intervened. Priam came alone to Achilles' tent with a ransom. He knelt and kissed the hands that had killed his sons. Achilles wept with him — for his own father, for Patroclus. He released the body. They ate together. The Trojans mourned Hector for nine days, and on the tenth they burned him. So they buried Hector, breaker of horses.
